title: Iran military vows to 'soon respond' after US destroyer hits cargo ship
subhed: US President Trump announced that the Iranian vessel, Touska, ignored warnings to stop in the Gulf of Oman and the USS Spruance 'stopped them right in their tracks by blowing a hole in the engine room.' Iran's military, later on Sunday, vowed to 'soon respond and retaliate,' reported the ISNA news agency.

Eight children dead in Louisiana domestic violence shooting, officials say
subhed: The gunman, who was not immediately identified but was an adult male, was fatally shot after a car chase and interaction with police on Sunday, Louisiana officials said. Police Corporal Chris Bordelon told a press conference that the deceased ranged in age from one to 14, at a 'rather extensive crime scene' that spanned three residences.

title: The undeniable international success of Languedoc wines
subhed: Appellations from the world's largest wine-growing region sell 40% of their production abroad. Thanks to strong ambassadors, wines from the area are now found in nearly half the countries on the planet.

title: 'Come Closer' by Tomora: When the Chemical Brothers' electronic fever meets Aurora's grace
subhed: British musician Tom Rowlands, one half of the Chemical Brothers, and Norwegian singer Aurora blend their musical worlds on their stunning first joint album.

title: From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
subhed: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.

subhed: A retrospective rich with vintage prints traces the career of this American force who constantly reinvented herself, from surrealist artist to war correspondent.

title: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
subhed: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.

title: Louis Verret paints the drama of football in watercolor
subhed: The artist based in Aubervilliers, a suburb of Paris, paints football's masters as if they are freeze-frames, channeling pure emotion straight to the goal.
